note: 06-03 window: copilot-sdk-session-auth ESCALATED — 100% of sdk-driver runs (3/3) failed the auth-info error, now on PRODUCTION main (2 wf) + 1 PR run. safe-output-partial-failure-intolerance recurred (target=* on Contribution Check). token-budget-429 ABSENT. 0 missing-tools/data/mcp.

issues.title: Did NOT recur in the 06-03 window (top eff_tok 9.97M Daily Safe Output Tool Optimizer << 25M cap). Stays OPEN #35661 — intermittent on heavy daily-aggregation workflows; light run-set this window.
issues.detail: 06-02 afternoon (14:31-17:23Z): NO 429 / no isMaxEffectiveTokensExceededError=true. Heaviest run UK AI Operational Resilience 23.79M succeeded under the 25M cap. This is the SECOND window-level reprieve (06-01 was also quiet) but the morning 06-02 window had 2 over-cap failures (Daily Firewall Logs 25.67M, Smoke Copilot 25.50M), so the cap stays intermittently hit by heavy daily-aggregation workflows. Maps to open #35661. FIX unchanged: (1) scope/chunk heavy daily-aggregation workflows; (2) harness FAIL-FAST on the maxEffectiveTokensExceeded signature instead of 5 retries.

issues.title: RECURRED 06-03 (target=* variant): Contribution Check agent SUCCEEDED & create_issue LANDED, but 3 add_comment items had target='*' with no item_number/pr_number specified -> 3 messages failed -> safe_outputs job RED despite the issue being created. add_labels(#aw_summary) then deferred unresolved. Stays OPEN/HIGH.
issues.detail: Night window added 2 variants. Changeset Generator 26847739151: push_to_pull_request_branch -> patch modifies files outside the allowed-files list (pkg/cli/codemod_pull_request_target_checkout_false.go) -> job red (security guard correct, no partial tolerance). Smoke Copilot 26847739375: dispatch_workflow haiku-printer -> No ref found for refs/heads/copilot/add-support-for-copilot-connection-token -> Message 3 failed -> job red while create_discussion etc. succeeded. Both on the same dev feature branch. Cumulative variants: target-triggering-not-in-context, set_issue_field no-issue-number, update_pull_request API branch-from-base, count-exceeded over-emission, allowed-files-list-rejection, dispatch_workflow no-ref-on-branch. FIX unchanged: make safe_outputs tolerate per-message failures and exit non-red when >=1 message lands and failures are expected/guarded. || 06-03: Contribution Check 26914897476 (main, schedule) emitted 5 safe items; Message 1 create_issue OK; Messages 2-4 add_comment failed: 'Target is "*" but no item_number/issue_number/pull_request_number specified in add_comment item' (the agent put the PR numbers only in the comment BODY text, not the target field); Message 5 add_labels deferred unresolved. 3 failed -> job red. Same root: one failed message red-fails the job even though >=1 landed. The target=* add_comment variant needs either (a) the agent to attach the PR number, or (b) safe_outputs to skip-and-warn unresolvable target=* comments rather than fail the job.

issues.title: CRITICAL ESCALATION (06-03): 100% of sdk-driver runs (3/3) failed 'Session was not created with authentication info or custom provider' — isAuthError=true, NOT retried (GH_TOKEN/COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN/GITHUB_TOKEN all absent/invalid). Now on PRODUCTION main (Copilot PR Prompt Pattern Analysis 26914529271, Daily Project Performance Summary Generator 26914622782) + a PR run (PR Code Quality Reviewer 26914463847). Legacy copilot path 0/14 fail. The sdk-driver rollout is broken: the SDK headless session is created WITHOUT auth being passed through.
issues.detail: 4 workflow_dispatch runs (26792092514, 26793757607, 26795908399, 26797319253) on experimental feature branch copilot/fix-copilot-sdk-integration all failed at the agent step with T0: copilot-sdk headless server starts (port 3002), sdk-driver connects + creates a session, then on 'sending prompt' throws uncaught 'Error: Execution failed: Error: Session was not created with authentication info or custom provider' and exits 1. This is an in-development experimental Copilot SDK integration being iterated via repeated manual dispatch (4 distinct SHAs over ~3h), NOT a production-workflow regression. Watch-only: confirm the fix lands and the SDK session is created WITH auth info / a custom provider before this graduates from smoke. The headless server warns 'No COPILOT_CONNECTION_TOKEN was set' — likely the missing auth-info path. | 06-02 afternoon: PR Code Quality Reviewer 26827796547 on branch copilot/ab-advisor-experiment-campaign-max-turns hit a NEW failure mode in the same experimental copilot-sdk family: sdk-driver 'Timeout after 600000ms waiting for session.idle' (10m hard), no output, not retried. Still experimental dev-iteration (ab-advisor max-turns campaign), not a production regression. Watch-only. | 06-02 EVENING ESCALATION: the SAME session.idle 600s timeout signature now hit a PRODUCTION scheduled workflow on main — Daily Security Observability Report 26836111514 (copilot, claude-sonnet-4.6). sdk-driver created the session, custom provider resolved WITH auth (provider=copilot baseUrl=api-proxy:10002), prompt sent, then 'Timeout after 600000ms waiting for session.idle', hasOutput=false, attempt-1 not retried (no-output rule), exit 1, 10m1s. NOT firewall-caused (only 1 trivial block patch-diff.githubusercontent.com). The copilot-sdk/sdk-driver path is now progressively rolled out to ~14/48 copilot+codex runs (~13 genuine prod copilot wfs this window: Daily SPDD Spec Planner, Daily Secrets Analysis, Daily Testify, GEO Optimizer, Linter Miner, PR Code Quality Reviewer x5, PR Triage, Daily Repository Chronicle, + the failing Security Observability Report). 1/~13 prod sdk runs hung => intermittent ~7% sdk-path hang; legacy copilot CLI path 0/34. FIX (High): (1) on the session.idle wait, after the 600s hard timeout RETRY at least once (the no-output non-retry rule is wrong for a session.idle hang — the session WAS created and the prompt WAS sent, so it is not a startup crash); (2) investigate why the headless Copilot server never emits session.idle for some prompts (possible long tool-loop or a lost SDK event); (3) until the sdk-driver session lifecycle is stable, consider keeping heavy/long production workflows on the legacy copilot path or lowering the rollout percentage. || 06-03 ESCALATION: this is no longer the experimental-branch session.idle TIMEOUT variant — it is the AUTH-INFO variant (same string as the 06-02 morning Smoke Copilot SDK runs) now hitting genuine production scheduled workflows on main and a production PR-trigger workflow. All 3 sdk-driver runs this window crashed at sdk-driver attempt 1 with node unhandled rejection 'Execution failed: Error: Session was not created with authentication info or custom provider', exit 1, turns=0, no output. The harness logs 'no authentication information found — not retrying (COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN, GH_TOKEN, and GITHUB_TOKEN are all absent or invalid)'. The headless copilot server starts (port 3002) but the SDK session creation does not receive a token/custom provider. FIX (CRITICAL): (1) HALT / roll back the copilot-sdk-driver rollout for production workflows until auth pass-through is fixed — every sdk-path run is currently a guaranteed failure; (2) ensure the sdk-driver creates the Copilot SDK session WITH the connection token / custom provider (wire COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN or a custom provider into copilot_sdk_driver.cjs session init); (3) keep production copilot workflows on the legacy copilot CLI path (0 failures) until the SDK session lifecycle + auth is verified end-to-end.

issues.title: NEW (infra): Daily Issues Report Generator failed agent step with exit 127 — 'node runtime missing on this runner'
issues.detail: Daily Issues Report Generator 26831132205 (copilot, schedule/main): agent step exit 127. The copilot_harness guard fired: 'node runtime missing on this runner — check runtimes.node in workflow YAML; exit 127'. The runner lacked a usable node binary (GH_AW_NODE_BIN unset/non-exec and `command -v node` empty). Pre-agent INFRA failure, not agent logic. Watch for recurrence — if it repeats, the workflow's runtimes.node setup or runner image is the culprit. Single occurrence this window.

issues.title: NEW: Avenger (claude/opus) ran 48.4min then failed with turns=0 / empty output — agent-step time-limit while still working
issues.detail: Avenger 26831111185 (claude, opus-4-8, schedule/main): agent job ran 15:47:48->16:36:13Z (48.4min) actively making opus /v1/messages calls and dispatching Bash + TaskOutput tools, then failed with run.Turns=0, ErrorCount=0, agent_output items:[]. Hit the agent step time limit (or a stuck long-running TaskOutput/Bash) without ever emitting a safe output. Watch: if Avenger recurs at ~48min, cap its turns/time or investigate a blocking TaskOutput poll. Single occurrence.

issues.title: NEW (infra): 2 agent-step failures pulling node:lts-alpine from registry-1.docker.io — context deadline exceeded, exit 123. Smoke CI (main) + Smoke Codex (branch) in the same night window.
issues.detail: Both Smoke CI 26849511621 and Smoke Codex 26847739078 failed the agent step at container-image pull: Get https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/ context deadline exceeded for node:lts-alpine, 3 retries then exit 123. Transient Docker Hub registry reachability/network friction during the run, NOT agent logic and NOT firewall-blocked. Two runs in one window (~21:05-21:39Z) suggests a brief registry slow window. WATCH recurrence; if persistent consider (1) caching/pinning the node base image in a warm layer, (2) a registry mirror, or (3) retry-with-backoff beyond 3 attempts on pull timeouts.
